TestPage.whyTestDescription: Knowing your level is essential to create <hs2>a learning plan</hs2> and define <hs4>goals</hs4>.
TestPage.whyTestAdditional: If you are considering taking one of <hs2>our courses</hs2>, we recommend completing this test to better target your skills.
TestPage.cefrLevelsDescription: The CEFR levels (A1 to C2) assess language proficiency from beginner to expert. They are <under>a universal reference</under> for learning and certification.

a1.title: Basic understanding of the language.
a1.content: You understand short and familiar statements when repeated. You can express basic needs.

a2.title: Mastery of fundamental language structures.
a2.content: You understand practical everyday information. You can make yourself understood in familiar situations.

b1.title: Limited proficiency in the language.
b1.content: You understand key information from texts and spoken content related to familiar situations. You can express yourself understandably on daily topics.

b2.title: General proficiency in the language.
b2.content: You understand detailed information in texts or spoken discourse on familiar, concrete, or abstract topics. You can express yourself clearly on subjects related to your interests.

c1.title: Strong command of the language.
c1.content: You understand complex texts and spoken content in detail on topics related to social and professional life. You express yourself confidently and precisely on various subjects.

c2.title: Full mastery of the language.
c2.content: You understand everything you read or hear across various domains. You grasp linguistic nuances and interpret complex documents with finesse. You express yourself spontaneously, accurately, and fluently, even on complex topics.

About.introDescription: I'm a teacher of French as a foreign language and a specialist in <noWrap>teaching beginners.</noWrap>
About.storyParagraph1: started as a primary school teacher before deciding to change my audience, quickly becoming an expert in teaching French as a foreign language. This allowed me the opportunity to work abroad as I went first to China, then to England and to New Zealand. In addition to working in many countries, I have also worked in a variety of different professional environments such as language schools (Alliance française), universities, and business schools where I was teaching general French to a demanding public <noWrap>of varied level.</noWrap>
About.storyParagraph2: Very early on, I specialized in teaching beginners as I always enjoyed seeing my students progress rapidly, being able to go from knowing no French to a level of confidence which allows them to survive in their environment and manage everyday situations. <noWrap>It's very rewarding.</noWrap>
About.storyParagraph3: In creating courses and materials online, I'm very happy to see that now my students come from different parts of the world. This drives me to always do my best to bring them answers and guide them toward <noWrap>their objectives.</noWrap>
About.storyParagraph4: I hope you will enjoy my courses and I look forward to hearing from you. Feel free to drop me a <noWrap>message anytime.</noWrap>

FideFAQ.title: <hs4>Frequently Asked Questions</hs4> about the FIDE
FideFAQ.subtitle: Find here the answers to the most common questions about the FIDE test.

qu_est_ce_que_fide.title: What is the FIDE?
qu_est_ce_que_fide.content: FIDE is a language test designed to assess language skills in French, German, or Italian in everyday situations in Switzerland. It is used for obtaining a residence or settlement permit and for naturalization.

content.part1: For a residence permit application (permit B), settlement permit (permit C), or naturalization, the requirements are different:

list.item1: A1 Oral level for permit B.
list.item2: A2 Oral level and A1 Written for permit C.
list.item3: B1 Oral level and A1 Written for early permit C.
list.item4: B1 Oral level and A2 Written for naturalization.

content.part2: Some cantons may require higher levels.

ou_et_quand_passer_examen.title: Where and when to take the FIDE test?

content.part1: The test can be taken at accredited centers in Switzerland. You can find the dates and locations on the official FIDE website or through local exam centers.

combien_de_temps_examen.title: How long does the FIDE test last?
combien_de_temps_examen.content: The oral test lasts about 40 minutes, and the written test lasts 60 minutes. Both parts can be taken separately.

parties_examen.title: What are the parts of the FIDE test?

content.part1: The FIDE exam is divided into two parts:

list.item1: Oral: Oral production (20 minutes) & Oral comprehension (20 minutes)
list.item2: Written: A combination of written comprehension and written production exercises (60 minutes)

combien_coute_examen.title: How much does the FIDE exam cost?
combien_coute_examen.content: The total cost is 250 CHF for both parts. If you only take the oral part, it costs 170 CHF, while the written part costs 120 CHF.

resultats_examen.title: When will I receive the results?
resultats_examen.content: The results are sent by mail approximately 4 weeks after the test. You will also receive a Language Passport.

validite_examen.title: How long is the FIDE test valid?
validite_examen.content: In principle, the FIDE Language Passport is valid for life.

nombre_de_passages.title: How many times can I take the FIDE exam?

content.part1: There is no limit to how many times you can take the test. You can retake it as many times as necessary to reach the desired level.
content.part2: Once one part (oral or written) is passed, you do not need to retake the entire exam, only the part you wish to improve.

difficulte_examen.title: Is the FIDE exam difficult?
difficulte_examen.content: Not really. It is easier to obtain FIDE B1 than another language test like DELF B1, for example. With some time and motivation, you should be able to achieve it, regardless of your starting level.

duree_obtention_b1.title: How long does it take to reach a B1 level?

content.part1: This depends on your initial level.
content.part2: For a complete beginner: Between 60 and 80 hours.
content.part3: For someone who already has an A2 level: Between 20 and 40 hours.
content.part4: These hours are based on the FIDE B1 level, which is easier than the official B1 level described by the CEFR, which requires more hours.

preparation_examen.title: How to best prepare for the FIDE test?

content.part1: The best method is to work with a teacher who knows the FIDE test well. They will help you establish a precise study plan based on your current level and the level you need to reach.
content.part2: Since the test is based on everyday life situations in Switzerland, the preparation should focus on that. Study the main recurring topics of the exam (Health / Housing / Work...), the essential grammar, and the Oral scenarios.

sujets_oral_examen.title: What are the Oral topics of the FIDE exam?

content.part1: The Oral topics are based on everyday situations for levels ranging from A1 to B1. The topics are therefore not unlimited, and certain themes often appear (Housing, neighbors, work, parties, health, transportation...).
content.linka: For more information on the Oral section and test topics,

meilleure_ecole_preparation.title: What is the best school to prepare for the FIDE test?

content.part1: Most schools specialized in the FIDE test offer good preparation.

list.item4: Access to the most recent/frequent test scenarios

FidePageHero.subtitle: Everything you need to know about the FIDE test, with videos for each section.

apply.suffix: and concrete tips for exam day.

p1.prefix: The FIDE test assesses your French in real-life situations in Switzerland. It is notably required for the

p2.prefix: You don't choose a level when registering: your result places you between
p2.suffix: . Results are split between oral and written, so you can retake only the part you didn't pass.

a1.title: Level A1
a1.text: Very simple communication in immediate situations.

b1.title: Level B1
b1.text: More developed responses in a range of situations.

FidePageStickyExams.sectionSubtitle: Three sections, three formats: discover what to expect and how to prepare effectively.

speaking.title: Oral section: Speaking
speaking.videoTitle: The FIDE test: Oral section - Speaking

paragraphs.p1: The “Speaking” part is the most important and demanding oral section of the FIDE test. It lasts about 15 to 20 minutes with two examiners: one asks questions, the other observes and evaluates. The section always starts at A2 level, then you are directed to A1 or B1 depending on your performance.
paragraphs.p2: If A2 is successful, you access B1 level. At this stage, you choose between two topics and answer more developed questions: recount a past experience, present pros and cons, and answer hypothetical questions (often using the conditional).
paragraphs.p3: Key skills to master are: clearly describing a situation, interacting in a common context, talking about yourself, recounting a past event, and developing a nuanced response at B1 level. This section represents a major part of oral evaluation, so preparation is strategic.
paragraphs.p4: Main advice: train with a teacher, especially to secure the A2 tasks that condition both your overall score and access to B1.

items.1: Image description: people, place, actions, and possible issue.
items.2: Role-play: often a phone conversation (canceling or rescheduling an appointment, etc.).
items.3: General questions on a theme to talk about habits, preferences, and experiences.

listening.title: Oral section: Listening
listening.videoTitle: The FIDE test: Oral section - Listening

paragraphs.p1: The “Listening” part evaluates oral comprehension across three possible levels: A1, A2 and B1. The proposed level depends on your performance in the previous oral section. The goal is to verify your ability to understand concrete everyday situations.
paragraphs.p2: Recommended strategies: ask for a second listen if needed, observe the illustrations carefully, trust your first impression, and review numbers, times and dates, which are very frequent in this section.
paragraphs.p3: Listening comprehension represents one third of the oral score, while speaking counts for the other two thirds.

items.a1: A1: short recordings linked to illustrated scenes, 3 questions per situation, answer by choosing the correct image. Audio can be played twice.
items.a2: A2: similar to A1 but with more complex situations, longer recordings, and distractors.
items.b1: B1: more natural and longer audios, oral response in full sentences based on what you heard.

readWrite.title: Reading and Writing
readWrite.videoTitle: The FIDE test: Reading and Writing

paragraphs.p1: The “Reading and Writing” part is generally more accessible than the oral section because tasks are more structured and predictable. The maximum level expected is often A2, which makes it more approachable for candidates who already have a base in French.
paragraphs.p2: Tasks are varied but clear: identify and copy information, fill out a form, answer an email or write a short letter, choose the right summary of a document.
paragraphs.p3: The goal is to evaluate practical reading comprehension and the ability to produce simple messages that are still appropriate.
paragraphs.p4: To progress, we recommend practicing with mock exams and their corrections, then repeating key formats: forms, extracting precise information, and writing short messages while strictly following instructions.
paragraphs.p5: In short, this section relies mainly on method, understanding instructions, and practical training, which often makes it easier to pass than the oral section.

items.modules: Modules: depending on the target level, modules 1 to 3 or modules 4 to 6.
items.perModule: In each module: reading comprehension + written production.

intro.line1: Preparing for FIDE is mostly about practicing the right situations and adopting a stable method.
intro.line2: arrive calm, understand instructions quickly, and answer precisely in both oral and written parts.

FidePageTips.videoTitle: The FIDE test: Tips and strategies

2.rest: : present, past, imperfect, future and conditional (B1).

3.rest: : use the second listen and focus on dates, times, numbers.

1.rest: , articulate, and ask to repeat if needed.

schema.serviceType: Private coaching for FIDE test preparation
schema.name: Private FIDE coaching online
schema.description: Personalized one-to-one coaching for FIDE test preparation (A1-A2 / A2-B1).

audience.title: Who are private FIDE courses for?
audience.content: These lessons are for learners preparing the FIDE test (A1-A2 or A2-B1), whether you are starting from zero or want to secure your level before exam day.

online.title: Are private lessons fully online?
online.content: Yes. All lessons are online, with step-by-step coaching and flexible scheduling.

scenarios.title: Do you train with current FIDE scenarios?
scenarios.content: Yes. We train with the most up-to-date scenarios based on feedback from students who have recently taken the exam.

hours.title: How many hours do I need?
hours.content: It depends on your current level, target, and timeline. If needed, we can help you define a clear and realistic study plan for free.
hours.contentLine1: It depends on your current level, target, and timeline.
hours.contentLine2: If needed, we can help you define a clear and realistic study plan for free.

quickBooking.title: Can I book quickly if my exam is soon?
quickBooking.content: Yes. Depending on availability, you can start quickly, including intensive last-minute preparation.

pack.title: Can I combine private lessons with the FIDE Pack?
pack.content: Yes. Private coaching pairs very well with the FIDE Pack: self-study between lessons, then targeted coaching on your weak points.

afterLesson.title: What do I get after each lesson?
afterLesson.content: After each lesson, you leave with clear priorities, concrete corrections, and focused exercises to keep improving.

getStarted.title: How do I get started?
getStarted.content: Book a free first call or send us your goal, current level, and exam date. We will recommend the best option for you.
getStarted.contentLine1: Book a free first call or send us your goal, current level, and exam date.
getStarted.contentLine2: We will recommend the best option for you.

PackFidePricing.privateDescription: For targeted 1:1 support: work on blocking points, fix mistakes live, and build confidence for the speaking part.

schema.productDescription: Structured FIDE video program, mock exams, and guided path to prepare with confidence.

faq.subtitle: Essential answers to choose the right e-learning option.

included.title: What is included in the FIDE e-learning pack?
included.content: The pack includes a structured video path (from beginner to B1), FIDE-specific content (method, key exam points, practical tips), 100+ mock exams, study resources, and regular updates. You also get a dashboard to track progress and a messaging space to exchange with your teacher.

difference.title: What is the difference between the self-paced and guided packs?
difference.content: The self-paced pack gives you all content for independent study. The guided pack adds closer pedagogical support, with targeted sessions on current FIDE scenarios.

fromZero.title: Is this suitable if I start from zero?
fromZero.content: Yes. The path is progressive and designed to take you step by step toward the expected FIDE level. If you are starting from scratch, you begin with the included beginner course, then move up to B1 through FIDE-specific content.

updates.title: Are scenarios updated regularly?
updates.content: Yes. Content is regularly updated based on recent candidate feedback and exam expectations. When new scenarios appear, we integrate them so your preparation stays aligned with the most relevant topics.

combine.title: Can I combine the pack with private coaching?
combine.content: Yes. Many learners combine e-learning with 1:1 coaching. The guided pack already includes closer support and targeted work on current FIDE scenarios. If you want even more personalized support, you can add private lessons.

freeOption.title: Is there a free option before purchasing?
freeOption.content: Yes. You can start with free videos and previews to test the method before buying.

speaking.title: Is the pack enough for speaking preparation as well?
speaking.content: Yes, the pack covers essential speaking foundations. If you need direct, personalized feedback, add supervised mock exams or private coaching.

choose.title: How should I choose between plans?
choose.content: If you are unsure which plan fits you best,
choose.tail: Your teacher can help you define a personalized plan based on your current level, timeline, and target.

Page.productDescription: Online mock exam very close to the FIDE test format: guided flow, instant score, detailed correction, PDF support, and videos.

standard.title: Standard plan
standard.description: Standard price for one mock exam (outside the discovery offer).

Faq.subtitle: Key points to know before starting your first mock exam.

format.title: Does the mock exam follow the official FIDE test format?
format.content: Yes. The mock exam is very close to the official FIDE test format, with similar instructions and flow. The goal is to put you in near-real conditions, reduce uncertainty, and help you understand exactly what to expect on test day.

parts.title: Which parts are included?
parts.content: All three parts are included: Speaking, Listening and Reading/Writing. The sequence is also respected to stay as close as possible to real FIDE test conditions.

tracks.title: Which tracks are available?
tracks.content: You can choose A1-A2 or A2-B1 depending on your objective. This mock test helps you place your level clearly and set a realistic progression target.

duration.title: How much time should I plan?
duration.content: Plan around 1 hour for a full session. Choose a calm moment without interruptions to get the most reliable result.

online.title: Is the full process fully online?
online.content: Yes. The full process is online from start to finish, with step-by-step guidance and immediate feedback at the end of your mock exam.

microphone.title: Do I need a specific microphone?
microphone.content: No. Your computer's built-in microphone is usually enough if the sound is clear. We still recommend a quiet environment. A written transcription is also provided so you can verify audio capture quality.

mobile.title: Can I take the test on mobile?
mobile.content: Yes, both mobile and desktop are possible. All Start French Now content is multi-device. For the test itself, desktop is still recommended for comfort.

pauseResume.title: What if I stop before finishing?
pauseResume.content: Your answers are saved progressively, step by step, which greatly limits data loss. You can stop and resume later without losing your work.

finalResult.title: What do I get at the end?
finalResult.content: As with the FIDE test logic, you get a success rate, a score, and an estimated level (A1/A2/B1) for each part, plus concrete improvement priorities.

pdfVideos.title: Are PDFs and videos included?
pdfVideos.content: Yes. The speaking part includes video support, and Reading/Writing includes PDF support, with immediate and permanent access.

retake.title: Can I retake the same mock exam?
retake.content: Yes, as many times as you want.

packDifference.title: What is the difference with the FIDE Pack?
packDifference.content: The mock exam is purchased as a standalone product. The FIDE Pack automatically includes broader Start French Now FIDE content: regularly updated current FIDE test scenarios, plus many trainings, French courses, and dedicated FIDE courses.

paymentMethods.title: Which payment methods are accepted?
paymentMethods.content: Secure Stripe checkout: card, Apple Pay, Google Pay, PayPal and Revolut.

contactAfterPurchase.title: How can I ask a question after purchase?
contactAfterPurchase.content: You can contact us via the dashboard messaging or by email at yohann@startfrenchnow.com.

Hero.imageAlt: A man looking at a book from which magical effluences emerge
Hero.title: <hs4>Immerse</hs4> Yourself in French Through an Interactive <hs2>Story</hs2>
Hero.description: Discover the benefits of interactive stories to progress in French. Improve your language skills in a fun and engaging way.

StoryTabs.title: <hs1>Test Your French Skills</hs1> Through An Interactive Story
StoryTabs.description1: Dive into our interactive stories and explore <hs4>the countless possibilities</hs4> they offer. Feel free to start over and unlock as many achievements as possible - but you'll never get them all! 😈
StoryTabs.description2: These journeys require a solid foundation in French, but don't worry if you're not quite there yet. We've got you covered with <hs2>helpful translations</hs2>. With a bit of dedication and a dash of creativity, you'll be making magic in no time.

WhyStories.title: Why Should You Learn French with <hs2>Interactive Stories</hs2>?
WhyStories.description: Unleash the power of interactive stories for an engaging and effective French learning experience. Here are the key benefits.

languageComprehension.title: Language comprehension
languageComprehension.description: Interactive stories help to understand the structure of the French language and the context in which words are used in a natural, intuitive way.

vocabularyAcquisition.title: Vocabulary acquisition
vocabularyAcquisition.description: These stories offer an excellent opportunity to acquire vocabulary in context, making learning more effective.

adaptedToYourNeeds.title: Adapted to your needs
adaptedToYourNeeds.description: Whatever your skill level, interactive stories adapt to your specific needs, enabling you to progress at your own pace.

engagementAndEnjoyment.title: Engagement and enjoyment
engagementAndEnjoyment.description: Interactive stories make learning French more engaging and enjoyable, thanks to an immersive and captivating experience.

repetitionAndReinforcement.title: Repetition and reinforcement
repetitionAndReinforcement.description: Repetition, a key element of language learning, is also facilitated by these stories.

anywhereAnytime.title: Anywhere, Anytime
anywhereAnytime.description: Their online accessibility means you can learn anytime, anywhere.

BetaVersion.title: <hs4>Help Us</hs4> Craft Your Ideal Learning Experience
BetaVersion.subtitle: <hs2>Speak Up, We're Listening</hs2>. Got an idea? Noticed something we could do better? We're all ears. Every piece of feedback is a step towards creating a more effective and enjoyable learning experience.

PrimaryPage.title: The Complete French Course : Learn French - <hs2>Beginners</hs2>
PrimaryPage.description: French Language for Beginners (A0 > A2). French speaking, listening, grammar, reading, vocabulary with a native teacher.

YouLearn.title: What <hs2>you will learn</hs2>
YouLearn.learn1: Master the basics of French for good!
YouLearn.learn2: Build and develop the practical communication skills in French: Speaking, Listening, Reading, and Writing.
YouLearn.learn3: Express yourself with ease on familiar topics.
YouLearn.learn4: Start to speak real French confidently. (A2 level)
YouLearn.learn5: Be confident regarding your French skills, use and understand everyday expressions.
YouLearn.learn6: Learn grammar and vocabulary with fun explanations and exercises.

Description.para1: This first course is probably the most important since it will give you a solid base of the French language and will for sure motivate you to speak it fluently.
Description.listItem1: You want to be serious about learning French (but with some fun, interactive and visually interesting exercises).
Description.listItem2: You need something structured (following The Common European Framework of Reference for Languages).
Description.listItem3: You want extra material after the lesson.
Description.listItem4: You are interested in getting A1 or A2 for DELF / TCF / TEF / FIDE ...
Description.para2: The course is divided in 28 lessons (16 hours of videos) all following a very logical progression which is based on many years of teaching and dozens of French methods. I also use the exact same material with my private students in one to one lessons and I can assure you that they improve !
Description.para3: Each lesson has a specific objective or theme and comes with vocabulary and grammar. All this new knowledge is tested through many exercises. For that part (exercises) you will need a pen and a piece of paper to do the exercises with me. It's easy, just follow the instructions on the video !
Description.para4: After each lesson you will have access to the summary of the lesson, extra exercises (with corrections) and a vocabulary list. So please, do your homework :)

IsForYou.title: Is This Course <hs2>Right for You</hs2>?
IsForYou.description: Make sure our course matches your needs and your current level of French. Feel free to contact us for any questions or for personalized advice before enrolling.
IsForYou.prerequisite1: You don't need any prior knowledge to start this course.
IsForYou.prerequisite2: The course is recommended to anyone who wants to learn French in a fast and efficient way.
IsForYou.prerequisite3: It's in French, don't worry: Immersion is the best method of learning a language.
IsForYou.prerequisite4: Native French speaking teacher with perfect accent.
IsForYou.levelDescription: At the end of this course you will have completed the A1.2 level and reach a low intermediate level of French (A2) allowing <noWrap>you to:</noWrap>
IsForYou.level1: understand sentences and frequently used expressions related to areas of most immediate relevance (e.g., basic personal and family information, shopping, local geography, employment).
IsForYou.level2: communicate in simple and routine tasks requiring a simple and direct exchange of information on familiar and routine matters.
IsForYou.level3: describe in simple terms aspects of your background, immediate environment, and matters in areas of immediate need.

LastComments.title: <hs2>Feedback</hs2> & Experiences
LastComments.description: Here's what our students say about this course. Form your own opinion on the quality of our teaching and the effectiveness of our method.

PrimaryPage.title: The Complete French Course : Learn French - <noWrap><hs4>Low Intermediate</hs4></noWrap>
PrimaryPage.description: French Language Course for Low Intermediate level A2 to B1. Advance your knowledge with a native teacher.

YouLearn.title: What <hs4>you will learn</hs4>
YouLearn.learn1: Deepen your knowledge of French and advance from A2 to B1
YouLearn.learn2: Learn all the tools you need for building longer and more complex sentences
YouLearn.learn3: Can deal with most situations while travelling in France
YouLearn.learn4: Learn the important French vocabulary and grammar for the level A2
YouLearn.learn5: Speak real French confidently on familiar topics
YouLearn.learn6: Start to express your opinion on different topics

Description.para1: First, If you are a complete beginner, please consider taking my first class :
Description.listItem1: You want to take your French to the next level.
Description.listItem2: If you finished my previous course and want to have a logical progression.
Description.listItem3: If you want to take the DELF A2 or a similar exam.
Description.listItem4: If you already learned some French in the past and would like to pursue the dream of speaking French 😉
Description.para2: The course is divided in 18 lessons (~12 hours of videos) all following a very logical progression which is based on many years of teaching and dozens of French methods. I also use the exact same material with my private students in one to one lessons and I can assure you that they improve !
Description.para3: Each lesson has a specific objective or theme using real-life situations to learn new vocabulary and grammar. All this new knowledge is tested through many exercises. For that part, you will need a pen and a piece of paper to do the exercises with me. It's easy, just follow the instructions on the video !
Description.para4: After each lesson you will have access to the summary of the lesson, extra exercises (with corrections) and a vocabulary list. So please, do your homework 😊

IsForYou.title: Is This Course <hs4>Right for You</hs4>?
IsForYou.description: Make sure our course matches your needs and your current level of French. Feel free to contact us for any questions or for personalized advice before enrolling.
IsForYou.prerequisite1: You should have a good knowledge of the French A1 vocabulary and grammar.
IsForYou.prerequisite2: Ideally my first course : The Complete French Course : Learn French - Beginners.
IsForYou.levelDescription: At the end of this course you will have completed the A2.1 level. You will need to finish the second part of this course (which I'm already working on) in order to finish the A2.2 level and reach the beginning of B1.

LastComments.title: <hs4>Feedback</hs4> & Experiences
LastComments.description: Here's what our students say about this course. Form your own opinion on the quality of our teaching and the effectiveness of our method.

PrimaryPage.title: The Complete French Course - <noWrap><hs5>Daily Life Conversations</hs5></noWrap>
PrimaryPage.description: French Language Course - Intermediate level A2 to B1. Master Everyday French Communication with 100 Real-Life Dialogues.

YouLearn.title: What <hs5>you will learn</hs5>
YouLearn.learn1: Master everyday life situations in French
YouLearn.learn2: Acquire essential French phrases for various communication contexts
YouLearn.learn3: Consolidate your skills with targeted exercises and assessments
YouLearn.learn4: Immerse yourself in over a hundred practical French dialogues
YouLearn.learn5: Increase your confidence practicing with real scenarios
YouLearn.learn6: Understand and use the subjunctive and conditional moods

Description.para1: First, If you are a complete beginner, please consider taking my first class :
Description.listItem1: You finished my previous courses and want a logical progression
Description.listItem2: You wish a course based on real everyday life scenarios
Description.listItem3: You want to know all the practical and useful expressions and speak like a local
Description.listItem4: You want to boost your confidence and be able to handle various situations in France
Description.para2: The course is divided in 40 lessons (~23 hours of videos and a hundred dialogues) all following a logical progression and exploring all the real-life situations you could encounter in France.
Description.para3: Each lesson starts with engaging dialogues on a specific topic, paired with questions to check your understanding. Then we break down each dialogue, highlighting key vocabulary and new grammar. We put all that into practice and finally we finish with a focus on communication, giving you the essential sentences to be able to deal with your daily French situations easily!
Description.para4: For the exercise part, don’t forget that you will need a pen and a piece of paper to complete them with me. It's easy, just follow the instructions on the video!
Description.para5: And very important ! After each lesson you will have access to the summary of the lesson and a vocabulary list with all the good expressions to use for each communication situation along with the translation and an audio document to ensure a perfect pronunciation !
Description.para6: You will also find bilans or assessments with many exercises through the course. So please, do your homework 😉

IsForYou.title: Is This Course <hs5>Right for You</hs5>?
IsForYou.description: Make sure our course matches your needs and your current level of French. Feel free to contact us for any questions or for personalized advice before enrolling.
IsForYou.prerequisite1: You should have an A2 level of French or strong A1.
IsForYou.prerequisite2: At least my first course : The Complete French Course : Learn French - Beginners
IsForYou.levelDescription: At the end of this course ranging from a A2 to B1 level, you will reach a conversational B1 level.

LastComments.title: <hs5>Feedback</hs5> & Experiences
LastComments.description: Here's what our students say about this course. Form your own opinion on the quality of our teaching and the effectiveness of our method.

PrimaryPage.title: Learn French - Intermediate <noWrap>level :</noWrap> Master <hs3>The Past Tenses</hs3>
PrimaryPage.description: Speak in the past in French - Learn the 3 main past tenses : Passé Composé - Imparfait - Plus-que-parfait.

YouLearn.title: What <hs3>you will learn</hs3>
YouLearn.learn2: Understand the differences between the past tenses and use them together
YouLearn.learn4: Master the 3 main tenses : Passé Composé - Imparfait - Plus-que-parfait
YouLearn.learn5: Practice with written and speaking exercises

Description.listItem1: You already have some knowledge of the French language (A1+/A2 level)
Description.listItem2: You would like something grammatically structured
Description.listItem3: You want to actively learn the Past Tenses (Do the exercises with me !)
Description.listItem4: You want to be able to express yourself in the past in French
Description.para2: The most important French past tenses are the Passé Composé and the Imparfait and they can be difficult to use for several reasons. They don't exactly have one english equivalent which make them tricky to translate. They also often work together and understanding the relationship between them is essential to communicating in French.
Description.para3: That's why, for each tense, we will see the different uses possible, the construction and finally we will practice with exercises.
Description.para4: Don't miss the summary and exercises sheets (with correction) that will help you practice and consolidate what you've just learned.
Description.para5: As a final assignment there is a project that you will be able to complete after attending this lesson. It is called "Une histoire d'amour - A love story" and you will find the Doc / PDF attached. I will gladly correct it and give you a personalized feedback. Use the summary of the lesson's PDF to help you with the verbs as well as the vocabulary list that is on the assignment sheet. Je suis impatient de lire vos histoires 😉
Description.content1: Le passé - Passé Composé - Imparfait - Plus-Que-Parfait
